questions.question: What exact JSON contract should `GET /v1/markets/{id}/meta` expose: a normalized preview object (for example `title`, `description`, `url`, `image`, `siteName`) or a raw map of HTML meta-tag names to values?
questions.why_it_matters: The endpoint does not exist yet, and the task only names the route. The response shape determines the backend handler, frontend types/client surface, and the test contract.

questions.question: What should the share-preview copy actually contain for a market: just the stored `title` and `description`, or should it include live state such as status, top probabilities, resolution, or expiry in the generated description/title?
questions.why_it_matters: The current market model already has several candidate fields for preview text. Without a canonical rule, the OG tags and `/v1/markets/{id}/meta` payload could drift or produce weak previews.

questions.question: Which meta tags are required for `/markets/{id}` HTML: only core Open Graph tags (`og:title`, `og:description`, `og:type`, `og:url`), or also `twitter:*`, standard `<meta name="description">`, and `og:image`?
questions.why_it_matters: There is no existing head-management layer in the frontend, so the implementation and test matrix depend on the minimum tag set that must be emitted server-side.

questions.question: How should absolute URLs be derived for preview metadata, especially `og:url` and any image URL: from the incoming request host/proto headers, from a fixed public origin such as `https://bayes.futarchy.ai`, or by some new config value?
questions.why_it_matters: Link-preview crawlers generally need absolute URLs, but the codebase does not currently expose a canonical public-origin configuration for market pages.

questions.question: Is an `og:image` required for this task, and if so, what asset or generation strategy should be used given that the repo currently has no checked-in image assets for Bayes Market?
questions.why_it_matters: An image materially affects link previews, but there is no existing logo/card asset or image-generation path in the repo. That choice changes both implementation scope and test expectations.

questions.question: What should a direct request to `/markets/{id}` do when the market id does not exist: continue serving the SPA shell with generic metadata so the client shows its current error state, or return a true HTTP 404 HTML/JSON response?
questions.why_it_matters: The backend currently falls back to `frontend/dist/index.html` for suffixless routes, so missing-market deep links already return the SPA shell. Changing that behavior affects UX, crawler behavior, and backend integration tests.

questions.question: Should the new meta endpoint be treated as a fully public documented API surface, meaning `GET /` should list it and the frontend API client/types should expose it, or is it only an internal helper for server-side HTML metadata generation?
questions.why_it_matters: The backend maintains a tested service-index route for public endpoints, and exposing the endpoint in shared client/types is a different contract commitment from keeping it backend-internal.

assumptions.assumption: Deep linking at the routing level already exists: the backend serves `frontend/dist/index.html` for `/markets/{id}` and the React router already defines `markets/:marketId`.
assumptions.if_wrong: The task would require new routing work instead of preserving the current SPA fallback and adding metadata on top of it.

assumptions.assumption: Market-specific OG tags need to be injected by the backend HTTP layer rather than by React alone, because the frontend is a client-only Vite app with no SSR/head management and crawlers will not wait for client-side fetches.
assumptions.if_wrong: A different server or rendering layer would own previews, and work in `backend/server.py` would be the wrong boundary.

assumptions.assumption: The new `/v1/markets/{id}/meta` route can be derived from the existing market read model (`id`, `title`, `description`, `status`, `outcomes`, `marginals`, `expires_at`) without introducing new persistence or inference primitives.
assumptions.if_wrong: Implementation scope expands beyond a thin read endpoint and HTML-tag generation into new data derivation or storage work.

assumptions.assumption: Checked-in `frontend/dist/` artifacts are part of the tested runtime surface and may need to remain updated if the HTML template changes, because backend integration tests currently serve those committed files directly.
assumptions.if_wrong: The change can stay source-only and rely on a build step outside this repo state, which would simplify the patch and reduce committed output churn.
